sources.name: DGT - Microdatos de Transferencias de Vehiculos (mensual/diario)
sources.yields: Raw per-transfer ownership-change records for all of Spain. Lets Cardeep detect active used-car traders (compraventas) by transaction patterns and geography, and size each trader's monthly volume. Companion microdata on the same portal: Matriculaciones (new registrations, dealer activity) and Bajas (deregistrations, feeds desguaces intelligence).
sources.est_volume: Monthly ZIP files, national scope; Spanish used-vehicle market is on the order of 2M transfers/yr (sector estimate, figure not stated on the page itself)
sources.notes: VERIFIED live 2026-06-12. Free ZIP download, no auth; programmatic download also via IEST web service (sedeapl.dgt.gob.es/IEST_INTER, descargaFicheroMicrodatos) and portal sedeapl.dgt.gob.es/WEB_IEST_CONSULTA. Since 2025-02-01 VIN/chassis is stripped (must request with legitimate interest via form). Record layout PDF ('diseno de registro') is linked on the page; whether it carries an explicit professional-seller/compraventa flag was NOT confirmed this session - verify against the layout PDF before relying on it.

sources.name: DGT - Centros Autorizados de Tratamiento de Vehiculos (CAT / desguaces)
sources.yields: The official, complete national list of authorized scrapyards (CATs) with province/municipality - the ground truth for the desguaces segment of the index.
sources.est_volume: Total not stated on page; sector body SIGRAUTO cites roughly 1,200-1,300 CATs nationally (unverified this session)
sources.notes: VERIFIED live 2026-06-12. Interactive map + list with province/municipality filters, last updated 2025-11-27; no bulk PDF/Excel export visible - scrape the underlying XHR/JSON behind the map per province. A datos.gob.es data request for this exact list (https://datos.gob.es/es/solicitud-de-datos/centros-autorizados-de-tratamiento-de-vehiculos-desguaces) is 'Asignado' to DGT as of 2026-03 but no open dataset published yet.

sources.name: BOE - BORME open data API (Registro Mercantil)
sources.yields: Daily commercial-registry acts: constituciones, ceses, disoluciones, cambios de objeto/domicilio (14 act types in Section I). Filter objeto social text for car-sales keywords to capture company births/deaths for dealers, compraventas, talleres, desguaces - the freshness signal for the index.
sources.est_volume: Daily summaries; ~100k new companies/yr across all sectors in Spain (car subset requires text filtering of act PDFs/XML)
sources.notes: VERIFIED live 2026-06-12. GET /datosabiertos/api/borme/sumario/{yyyymmdd}, XML and JSON, no auth or rate limits documented; XSD schemas provided; per-act documents in PDF (Section II also HTML/XML). FAQ at boe.es/datosabiertos/faq/borme.php. Pre-parsed alternatives seen: rOpenSpain/BOE (GitHub), openmercantil.es.

sources.name: INE - DIRCE / Estadistica Estructural de Empresas (counts by CNAE)
sources.yields: Official company COUNTS by CNAE group and region: 451 venta de vehiculos, 452 mantenimiento y reparacion, 454 motocicletas (confirmed present in table selector); 4-digit indicators (4511, 4519, 4520, 4540, 4677) via EEE tables t=24889 / t=25041. No company names (DIRCE microdata is confidential) - use as the coverage denominator / QA benchmark for Cardeep, not as an entity source.
sources.est_volume: Counts only, no entity records; full regional x CNAE-group matrix downloadable
sources.notes: VERIFIED live 2026-06-12 (table config page; groups 451/452/454 listed; export via dlgExport.htm?t=39372). Programmatic access via INE Tempus3 JSON API (servicios.ine.es/wstempus). Actual national figures not extracted this session - pull them when setting QA targets.

sources.name: Iberinform - Directorio de empresas por CNAE
sources.yields: Free browsable list of company name + province + municipality for the entire vehicle-retail CNAE; the fetched page (rendered under the new CNAE-2025 code 4781, comercio al por menor de vehiculos de motor) showed 26,205 companies in its breadcrumb - the single best free enumeration of car-sales companies seen this session.
sources.est_volume: 26,205 companies under retail motor-vehicle CNAE (count seen on fetched page 2026-06-12)
sources.notes: VERIFIED live 2026-06-12. Simple numbered pagination; also a 'Directorio Geografico' view. Deeper data (CIF, financials) is paid (Iberinform/Crédito y Caución). Site is migrating CNAE 2009 -> CNAE 2025 codes, so enumerate both code systems. Anti-bot posture untested at volume - assume rate limiting.

sources.name: eInforma - Directorio de empresas por CNAE + informes sectoriales
sources.yields: Free per-CNAE company lists with name, city, province and website (40/page); free sector reports give official-style counts, e.g. CNAE 4671 wholesale of motor vehicles = 3,489 companies with revenue breakdown by region.
sources.est_volume: 3,489 companies CNAE 4671 wholesale (stated in fetched sector report); retail CNAE pages paginated 40/page x 17+ pages on the listing fetched
sources.notes: VERIFIED live 2026-06-12 (fetched CNAE-4511 listing URL, which now serves CNAE-2025 content - same migration caveat as Iberinform). Listing pages free; CIF/financials paid. eInforma is also the data provider behind elEconomista's ranking pages.

sources.notes: VERIFIED live 2026-06-12. 30 results/page, numbered pagination, browsable by all 52 provinces. Keyword taxonomy, not strict CNAE - good for contact enrichment rather than exhaustive enumeration.

sources.name: camoufox (daijro/camoufox)
sources.yields: Stealth Firefox build with C++-level fingerprint spoofing (canvas, WebGL, fonts, navigator, screen). Primary engine for rendering JS-heavy Spanish POS sites (dealer locators, used-car portals) behind Cloudflare/Turnstile and fingerprint walls.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE/very active. 9,169 stars. Latest release v150.0.2-beta.25 (2026-05-11), repo pushed 2026-06-10. 256 open issues (active triage).
sources.notes: VERIFIED via GitHub API. Best-in-class for fingerprint-level evasion; pairs natively with Scrapling and browserforge. Still beta-tagged but mainstream. Top recommendation for the browser tier.

sources.name: curl_cffi (lexiforest/curl_cffi)
sources.yields: Python HTTP client with JA3/JA4 + HTTP2 + Akamai TLS fingerprint impersonation of real browsers. Fast no-browser path for hitting JSON/XHR endpoints of Spanish portals (coches.net, dealer APIs) without paying browser cost.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE/very active. 5,801 stars. Latest release v0.15.1b2 (2026-06-05), repo pushed 2026-06-07.
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. Repo was renamed from yifeikong/curl_cffi -> lexiforest/curl_cffi (old URL 301-redirects, same repo). Core workhorse for the no-browser tier; beats most TLS defenses cheaply.

sources.name: curl-impersonate (lexiforest fork)
sources.yields: The patched C curl/BoringSSL backend that curl_cffi wraps. Direct CLI/lib use for TLS+HTTP2 impersonation in non-Python stacks.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE. 2,564 stars. Repo pushed 2026-06-03 (fork of lwthiker/curl-impersonate).
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. IMPORTANT: the ORIGINAL lwthiker/curl-impersonate is effectively DEAD (last push 2024-07-18, ~23 months stale). lexiforest fork is the maintained successor. Use the fork, not the original.

sources.name: Scrapling (D4Vinci/Scrapling)
sources.yields: High-level adaptive scraping framework: StealthyFetcher (camoufox), PlayWrightFetcher, and TLS Fetcher in one API, plus self-healing selectors that survive site redesigns. Ideal orchestration layer for a multi-source Spain POS pipeline.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE/extremely active. 63,184 stars (largest in this census). Latest release v0.4.9 (2026-06-07), repo pushed 2026-06-07. Only 5 open issues.
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. Bundles camoufox + browserforge under the hood and exposes adaptive selectors — strong default framework to standardize the Cardeep arsenal on.

sources.name: patchright (python) (Kaliiiiiiiiii-Vinyzu/patchright-python)
sources.yields: Drop-in undetected Playwright patch (removes Runtime.enable/CDP leaks, closes console/navigator tells). Lets existing Playwright scraper code pass Cloudflare/DataDome with minimal rewrite.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE. 1,383 stars. Latest release v1.60.0 (2026-06-03), repo pushed 2026-06-09. 1 open issue.
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. Tracks upstream Playwright versions closely; the modern replacement for the dead playwright-stealth approach. Top pick if the team already has Playwright code.

sources.name: patchright (nodejs) (Kaliiiiiiiiii-Vinyzu/patchright-nodejs)
sources.yields: Node/TypeScript build of the undetected Playwright patch. Same evasion for JS-stack scrapers.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE. 713 stars. Repo pushed 2026-06-09. 0 open issues.
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. Use only if the pipeline is Node-based; otherwise prefer the Python build.

sources.name: nodriver (ultrafunkamsterdam/nodriver)
sources.yields: Official successor to undetected-chromedriver: direct CDP control, no Selenium/webdriver, async, hard to fingerprint. Solid general-purpose stealth browser for difficult Spanish targets.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE. 4,350 stars. Repo pushed 2026-05-13. Ships releases via PyPI (no GitHub Releases — API 404 is expected, not a death signal).
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. Cadence is a bit slower than camoufox/Scrapling but stable. Same author as undetected-chromedriver, which this replaces.

sources.name: zendriver (cdpdriver/zendriver)
sources.yields: Actively maintained fork of nodriver with extra fixes, typing, and faster issue response. Same direct-CDP stealth browsing for hard targets.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE. 1,318 stars. Latest release v0.15.3 (2026-03-12), repo pushed 2026-04-19. 70 open issues (engaged community).
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. Repo moved from stephanlensky/zendriver -> cdpdriver org (old URL redirects). Choose between nodriver and zendriver; zendriver tends to merge community fixes faster.

sources.name: primp (deedy5/primp)
sources.yields: Rust-backed Python HTTP client with browser TLS/JA3 + header-order impersonation. Lightweight alternative to curl_cffi for high-volume no-browser fetching.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE. 538 stars. Latest release v1.3.1 (2026-05-23), repo pushed 2026-06-09.
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. Smaller community than curl_cffi but actively maintained; viable secondary TLS client for rotation/diversity.

sources.name: botasaurus (omkarcloud/botasaurus)
sources.yields: All-in-one anti-detect scraping framework (browser + request modes, caching, parallelism, built-in Cloudflare handling, UI generator). Rapid-build option for standing up a Spain POS scraper quickly.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE but slower cadence. 4,796 stars. Repo pushed 2026-03-18 (~3 months before census). Releases via PyPI (no GitHub Releases).
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. Healthy but commit cadence trails the front-runners; fine as a framework, less cutting-edge on evasion than camoufox/patchright.

sources.name: SeleniumBase (seleniumbase/SeleniumBase) — UC/CDP Mode
sources.yields: Mature test/scrape framework whose UC Mode and CDP Mode reliably clear Cloudflare Turnstile and similar. Battle-tested, heavily documented, huge user base — low-risk choice for production Cardeep crawling.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE/very active. 12,781 stars. Repo pushed 2026-06-12 (day of census). Only 14 open issues.
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. Was missing from the original list but is a first-tier Cloudflare/Turnstile tool. Byparr uses it internally. Strong, well-supported default.

sources.name: FlareSolverr (FlareSolverr/FlareSolverr)
sources.yields: Standalone proxy service that solves Cloudflare/anti-bot challenges and returns cookies+HTML; language-agnostic, sits in front of any scraper. Drop-in unblock service for the pipeline.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE again. 14,270 stars. Latest release v3.5.0 (2026-05-26), repo pushed 2026-06-05. 74 open issues.
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. Had a long stall period; v3.5.0 indicates renewed activity. Weaker against modern Cloudflare Turnstile than camoufox/SeleniumBase — keep Byparr as the alternative.

sources.name: Byparr (ThePhaseless/Byparr)
sources.yields: Drop-in FlareSolverr-API-compatible successor built on SeleniumBase/nodriver. Same proxy-unblock contract with a more modern solver core. Direct swap for stacks already calling FlareSolverr.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE. 1,601 stars. Latest release v2.1.0 (2026-02-08), repo pushed 2026-06-11. 25 open issues.
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. The named FlareSolverr successor; keeps the same /v1 API so it slots in without client changes. Recommended unblock-service tier alongside SeleniumBase.

sources.name: browserforge (daijro/browserforge)
sources.yields: Intelligent header + browser-fingerprint generator (realistic, internally-consistent User-Agent/Accept/sec-ch-ua sets via Bayesian model). The modern replacement for fake-useragent; feeds realistic headers to curl_cffi/requests.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE. 1,130 stars. Repo pushed 2026-02-26 (~3.5 months before census). Releases via PyPI.
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. Used internally by camoufox and hrequests. Recommend this over the archived fake-useragent for UA/header generation.

sources.name: fake-useragent (fake-useragent/fake-useragent) — ARCHIVED/DEAD
sources.yields: Random User-Agent string provider from a bundled dataset. Still importable but no longer updated.
sources.est_volume: DEAD/ARCHIVED. 4,058 stars. Archived (read-only) by owner on Apr 1, 2026. Last release 2.2.0 (2025-04-14).
sources.notes: VERIFIED: archive banner reads 'This repository was archived by the owner on Apr 1, 2026. It is now read-only.' No official successor named. DO NOT adopt for new work — use browserforge instead. UA-string-only spoofing is also insufficient against modern fingerprinting.

sources.name: playwright-stealth (Mattwmaster58/playwright_stealth)
sources.yields: Maintained fork that owns the current playwright-stealth PyPI package: JS evasion patches for vanilla Playwright. Light-touch hardening when patchright is not an option.
sources.est_volume: Semi-active. 249 stars. Repo pushed 2026-04-04 (~2 months before census).
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. IMPORTANT: the widely-cited ORIGINAL AtuboDad/playwright_stealth is DEAD (last push 2024-07-29, ~22 months stale). The JS-evasion approach is largely outclassed by patchright/camoufox — prefer those; keep this only as a fallback.

sources.name: hrequests (daijro/hrequests) — STALE
sources.yields: Hybrid HTTP client + headless browser with TLS impersonation and HTML parsing in one package. Conceptually convenient but no longer kept current.
sources.est_volume: STALE/DORMANT. 1,009 stars. Last release/commit 2024-12-01 (~18 months before census). No deprecation notice in README, but zero commits since.
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API + README fetch (no 'deprecated' wording, but ~18 months with no activity). Treat as effectively unmaintained — its impersonation profiles will drift behind current browsers. Prefer curl_cffi + camoufox/Scrapling instead.

sources.name: ulixee/hero (ulixee/hero)
sources.yields: Full-stack 'scraper browser' with emulated human profiles and a high-level client/DOM API. Ambitious but perpetually pre-release.
sources.est_volume: Slow/alpha. 1,544 stars. Latest release v2.0.0-alpha.34 (2025-09-08, ~9 months before census), repo pushed 2026-05-16.
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. Still committed to but stuck in long-running alpha; heavier and less proven than camoufox/nodriver for production. Low priority for Cardeep.

sources.name: undetected-chromedriver (ultrafunkamsterdam/undetected-chromedriver) — LEGACY
sources.yields: The classic Selenium-based stealth Chromedriver. Historically the default anti-bot browser; now superseded.
sources.est_volume: Declining/maintenance-limbo. 12,682 stars. Last push 2025-07-05 (~11 months before census). 1,143 open issues (large backlog).
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API. Same author explicitly moved effort to nodriver. Large unmaintained issue backlog; webdriver-based detection is increasingly caught. Use nodriver/zendriver instead — listed here only to flag it as legacy.

sources.name: BotBrowser (botswin/BotBrowser)
sources.yields: Patched Chromium core with unified fingerprint defense advertised against Cloudflare, Akamai, Kasada, DataDome, PerimeterX, Imperva and others. Heavy-duty engine for the hardest Spanish targets (e.g. Akamai-protected OEM/finance portals).
sources.est_volume: ALIVE. 2,489 stars. Repo pushed 2026-06-10. Only 4 open issues.
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API + surfaced in GitHub anti-bot search. Broadest defense coverage (incl. Akamai/DataDome/PerimeterX) of any tool here. Distributed as patched-browser binaries — evaluate licensing/operational fit before adopting.

sources.name: browsers-benchmark (techinz/browsers-benchmark)
sources.yields: Reproducible benchmark of bypass/success rates and performance across engines (camoufox, nodriver, patchright, SeleniumBase, etc.) vs Cloudflare, DataDome, Akamai, PerimeterX, Kasada, reCAPTCHA. Decision-support to pick the right tool per Spanish source's defense.
sources.est_volume: ALIVE. 324 stars. Repo pushed 2026-06-09. 1 open issue.
sources.notes: VERIFIED via API + search. Not a scraper itself — a current empirical reference for choosing engines. Useful to ground Cardeep's per-source tool routing in data rather than reputation.

sources.name: is-antibot (microlinkhq/is-antibot)
